description.en: The concatenation of the drive and root: >>> PureWindowsPath('c:/Program Files/').anchor 'c:\\' >>> PureWindowsPath('c:Program Files/').anchor 'c:' >>> PurePosixPath('/etc').anchor '/' >>> PureWindowsPath('//host/share').anchor '\\\\host\\share\\'

info.excerpt: The concatenation of the drive and root: >>> PureWindowsPath('c:/Program Files/').anchor 'c:\\' >>> PureWindowsPath('c:Program Files/').anchor 'c:' >>> PurePosixPath('/etc').anchor '/' >>> PureWindows...

description.en: Make the path absolute, without normalization or resolving symlinks. Returns a new path object: >>> p = Path('tests') >>> p PosixPath('tests') >>> p.absolute() PosixPath('/home/antoine/pathlib/tests')

info.excerpt: Make the path absolute, without normalization or resolving symlinks. Returns a new path object: >>> p = Path('tests') >>> p PosixPath('tests') >>> p.absolute() PosixPath('/home/antoine/pathlib/tests')

description.en: Return a string representation of the path with forward slashes (/): >>> p = PureWindowsPath('c:\\windows') >>> str(p) 'c:\\windows' >>> p.as_posix() 'c:/windows'

info.excerpt: Return a string representation of the path with forward slashes (/): >>> p = PureWindowsPath('c:\\windows') >>> str(p) 'c:\\windows' >>> p.as_posix() 'c:/windows'

description.en: Represent the path as a ‘file’ URI. ValueError is raised if the path isn’t absolute. >>> p = PosixPath('/etc/passwd') >>> p.as_uri() 'file:///etc/passwd' >>> p = WindowsPath('c:/Windows') >>> p.as_uri() 'file:///c:/Windows' Deprecated since version 3.14, will be removed in version 3.19: Calling this method from PurePath rather than Path is possible but deprecated. The method’s use of os.fsencode() makes it strictly impure.

info.excerpt: Represent the path as a ‘file’ URI. ValueError is raised if the path isn’t absolute. >>> p = PosixPath('/etc/passwd') >>> p.as_uri() 'file:///etc/passwd' >>> p = WindowsPath('c:/Windows') >>> p.as_uri...
